title: D'Arcy Concession (1901) and its 1933 Renegotiation -- Compiled Terms
source_org: Compiled by this project from multiple secondary/academic sources (curated, hand-compiled -- see docs/bookkeeping.md rule 5)
source_url: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/D'Arcy_Concession ; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anglo-Persian_Oil_Company ; WebSearch aggregation of Encyclopaedia Iranica 'OIL AGREEMENTS IN IRAN', Encyclopedia.com, Foreign Affairs (1933)
time_coverage: 1901-1993 (concession terms and their 1933 renegotiation; actual nationalization in 1951 ends the arrangement early, already covered by this project's existing oil-nationalization sources)
frequency: n/a (structured term sheet, not a time series)
units: see data.csv unit column per row
license_terms: compiled from multiple sources cited per-row
notes: This is technically an OIL concession (not the mission's primary 'non-oil FDI' focus) but was explicitly requested by name in the mission brief ('the D'Arcy/APOC concession terms specifically if not already covered'). Checked first: the project's existing Iranica/telecom-history mentions of D'Arcy were narrative-only, no structured terms dataset existed. This fills that gap with the actual concession numbers: 1901 terms (60-year duration, 1,242,000 sq km territory, GBP 20,000 cash + GBP 20,000 shares upfront, 16% of net profits royalty, 2-year company-formation deadline) plus the 1933 renegotiation that replaced it (ratified 28 May 1933, extended to 1993, fixed 4-shillings-per-ton royalty replacing the disputed 16%-of-profits formula, 20% of distributed profits above a minimum, GBP 750,000 guaranteed minimum annual payment, concession area cut from 480,000 sq mi to 100,000 sq mi by 1938). Both sets of terms cross-checked across 2+ independent sources (Wikipedia D'Arcy Concession page + Wikipedia Anglo-Persian Oil Company page + WebSearch aggregation citing Encyclopaedia Iranica/Encyclopedia.com/Foreign Affairs 1933) and found consistent -- no contradictions requiring a range/flag.
